scenario_summary: A private mirror tries to republish marketplace metadata without preserving the signed index. versioning.marketplace_metadata_schema declares versioning_model_class content_addressed_with_signed_index and compatibility_capability_source signed_index_capability_table; the marketplace_metadata_signed_index invariant refuses any republish that weakens the signed index. The rule mirrors ADR 0012 (mirrors and private registries MUST preserve digests, signatures, compatibility metadata, and permission manifests).

explanation_fields.user_facing_summary: Mirrors and private registries MUST preserve the signed index that ties marketplace metadata names to verifiable bytes. A mirror that drops or weakens the signed index is refused.
explanation_fields.release_notes_summary: Marketplace metadata is content-addressed with a signed index; consumers resolve logical names through the signed index. Mirrors that weaken the signed index are non-conforming.
explanation_fields.support_summary: Support packets cite the marketplace_metadata_signed_index invariant and the customer-managed-mirror install card under review so reviewers can confirm the refusal is mechanical.
explanation_fields.promotion_summary: Refuse. The signed index is the negotiation surface; weakening it would silently widen what the mirror may serve.

notes: Reference fixture for the marketplace_metadata_signed_index invariant on artifact_family_versioning.yaml.
